title: Movies
text: Rotten Tomatoes publishes two scores for every movie: the Tomatometer, which is the percentage of approved critics who gave a positive review, and the audience score, which comes from verified ticket buyers who rate films on a 0–5 scale converted to a percentage. The same movie can score very differently on each scale. This analysis uses a dataset of 1,000 movies to ask: do critics and audiences systematically disagree, and which genres drive the biggest gap between their scores?

title: About
text: I am Var Kurapati, a data science intern working with the PPBDS project. I investigated the critic-audience gap on Rotten Tomatoes because I was curious whether the disconnect between critics and general audiences is real and systematic, or just anecdotal. The data shows that genre plays a significant role — action and family films consistently score higher with audiences, while documentaries are rated more favorably by critics.
